title: How to calculate CAGR (compound annual growth rate)
task: Work out the average yearly growth rate between a starting and ending value.

excel.note: A2=start value, B2=end value, C2=years. Or the built-in =RRI(C2,A2,B2).

explanation: CAGR is (end/start)^(1/years) − 1: doubling in 10 years is 2^(1/10) − 1 ≈ 7.18% per year — the steady rate that would produce the same result. Format as a percentage. It smooths over the bumps by design, which is both its point and its limitation: volatile year-to-year paths with the same endpoints give the same CAGR. RRI(periods,start,end) is the same math built in.
